metadata.title: Anshika's Algorithm That Stopped Shouting
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Anshika Khandelwal
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Anshika Khandelwal — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The algorithm filters a noisy information stream specifically for investment-relevant keywords. It structures that chaos into signal, turning scattered inputs into something usable for decisions. Stopping shouting means the system became more selective, cutting noise so only meaningful outputs surface. Built within the Humanitarians AI Fellows Program, it embodies the principle that the work itself needs to matter.

artifactLines: The algorithm filters a noisy information stream specifically for investment-relevant keywords.
It structures that chaos into signal, turning scattered inputs into something usable for decisions.
Stopping shouting means the system became more selective, cutting noise so only meaningful outputs surface.
Built within the Humanitarians AI Fellows Program, it embodies the principle that the work itself needs to matter.

beats.narration_text: The image of an algorithm that stopped shouting, one that filters for investment keywords and structures chaos into signal, is a design goal I want to make precise. The insistence that the work needs to matter frames why signal beats noise here. Can you (1) specify how keyword filtering plus structuring turns a noisy stream into usable investment signal, (2) explain what 'stopped shouting' means as a change in precision or noise reduction, and (3) sketch one evaluation that proves the algorithm actually surfaces what matters?

output: You wonder if anyone is even reading these
It reads every new article published in the last 24 hours
The tool saves more than ten hours per week

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'Anshika's Algorithm That Stopped Shouting' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.
